title: Simplicity Scales

content.question: What's one thing you overcomplicated that you can cut in half this week?
sop: List every step in a process or offer that currently exists.
Rate Simplicity, fewer moving parts, and Consistency, how reliably you follow it, each 1-10 (see calculation, page 4).
Cut any step that doesn't directly serve the core outcome.
Rebuild the process with the fewest possible steps that still work.
Run it for 2 weeks before adding anything back, most cuts don't need replacing.
questions: What's one thing you overcomplicated that you can cut in half this week?
If you had to explain your offer or process to a new hire in one sentence, could you?
Which step in your business exists only because you added it once and never questioned it again?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: Simplicity 9, Consistency 7. Score = 9 times 7 = 63 out of 100. Higher means the plan is simple enough to actually stick to; a complex plan, low Simplicity, will drag this number down even with high effort.
content.closing_statement: The simplest version is usually the one that ships.

caption:
Principle Nº 115: Simplicity Scales

Complexity kills execution.

Ask yourself: What's one thing you overcomplicated that you can cut in half this week?
